title: The Config Hunt (Capstone)
scenario: A server was handed to you with no documentation and no handover notes. You know it runs an application called myapp, but nothing else. Before the team can decide whether to keep the server running or decommission it, they need four pieces of information extracted from the filesystem. This is a capstone lab combining find, grep, and tail.
symptoms: Undocumented server with no handover notes
Unknown database connection target (DB_HOST not documented)
mission: Find all .conf files under /etc/myapp/, extract the DB_HOST line from the config, count ERROR lines in /opt/myapp/app.log, and capture the last 10 lines of /opt/myapp/deploy.log.

hints.title: Wildcards in find
hints.content: Use *.conf as the -name pattern to match any file ending in .conf. Make sure to quote the pattern so the shell does not expand it before passing it to find.

hints.title: Extracting a config value
hints.content: 'grep KEY /path/to/file' prints the full line containing that key. If you are not sure which file contains it, search the whole directory with -r to scan all files at once.

hints.title: Counting errors
hints.content: 'grep -c PATTERN file' returns a single number: the count of lines that match. Redirect that number directly to your output file with >.

hints.title: Recent log entries
hints.content: 'tail -10 file' prints the last 10 lines. No flags other than the line count are needed — this is a simple, one-shot read.

hints.title: Bonus
hints.content: Try chaining find and grep together using xargs. This lets you search for a config value across all matching files at once without needing to know their names in advance.

acceptance_criteria.description: /home/intern/conf_files.txt exists and contains paths to .conf files under /etc/myapp/
/home/intern/db_host.txt exists and contains the DB_HOST line from the myapp config
/home/intern/error_count.txt exists and contains a number greater than 0
/home/intern/deploy_tail.txt exists and contains the last 10 lines of /opt/myapp/deploy.log
